data.title: Fortis Capital Advisors named #2 Fastest-Growing RIA and Top Firm for 2025 by Financial Advisor Magazine.
data.description: Fortis Capital Advisors, located in Prairie Village, Kansas, was recently recognized by Financial Advisor Magazine as the second fastest-growing Registered Investment Advisor (RIA) in the United States for 2025. Additionally, Fortis was named one of America's Top RIAs for the same year. This prestigious acknowledgment highlights Fortis' dedication to excellence in the financial advisory sector.

data.title: ASTHO maps state suicide prevention infrastructure, in partnership with Temple University and CDC, releasing interactive resource.
data.description: On July 24, 2025, in Arlington, Virginia, the Association of State and Territorial Health Officials (ASTHO) revealed a new interactive resource in collaboration with Temple University and backed by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC). This resource aims to provide vital information to the public, with a focus on health and disease prevention. The collaboration between the organizations highlights the importance of such initiatives in enhancing public health awareness.
2025 first-half revenues for Marie Brizard Wine & Spirits are out, showing positive growth.

data.title: U.S. economy cautiously optimistic for second half of 2025 - Payden & Rygel projects growth, Fed likely to resume interest rate cuts amid cooling inflation, job growth. Fed may act if inflation cools further or unemployment rises.
data.description: The economics team at Payden & Rygel in Los Angeles, in their Mid-Year Macro Outlook for 2025, remains cautiously optimistic about the U.S. economy despite a volatile start to the year. They anticipate continued growth, with a prediction of moderating inflation and the Federal Reserve likely to implement interest rate cuts before the end of the year. The markets experienced upheaval due to unexpected tariffs in April and concerns about a looming recession. However, with inflation stabilizing and job growth remaining solid, Payden foresees a less severe economic downturn. Jeffrey Cleveland, the Chief Economist at Payden & Rygel, stated that while markets reacted strongly to the April tariff developments, data suggests support for rate cuts and sustained growth. Looking ahead to the second half of 2025, the firm believes that if inflation continues to decrease or unemployment sees a slight increase, the Fed will take action. This analysis indicates a potential shift towards a more stable economic climate.

data.title: Spotify stock rises after analyst upgrade ahead of Q2 earnings.
data.description: Shares of Spotify Technology saw an increase in value on Thursday following a favorable assessment from a financial analyst. The stock surge occurred in anticipation of the company's second-quarter earnings report.

data.title: Holland America Line improves entertainment with new shows, more live music, and cultural activities. Evolved experience for guests.
data.description: Holland America Line will introduce enhanced onboard entertainment experiences for guests, including a new 'Welcome Aboard' show and updated World Stage production shows. The offerings will also include a greater selection of live music performances across multiple venues and additional cultural and regional activities. The changes will be implemented starting in July 2025 in Seattle.

data.title: AtWork Group shows robust growth in first half of 2025, expands with new locations and earns industry honors.
data.description: A staffing franchise headquartered in Knoxville, Tennessee, AtWork®, has recently expanded by opening new locations. The company, which has received top honors in the staffing industry, revealed impressive growth and achievements in the first six months of 2025. As of July 24, 2025, AtWork® has established itself as a key player in the industry, showcasing its commitment to excellence and strategic development.

data.title: Nominations open for 2026 Real Estate Newsmakers by RISMedia. Celebrating individuals shaping real estate future.
data.description: Norwalk, Connecticut - On July 24, 2025, RISMedia announced that nominations are open for the 2026 Class of Real Estate Newsmakers. This program aims to recognize individuals who are influencing the real estate industry with their innovative ideas, effective leadership, and dedication to service. The Real Estate Newsmakers initiative highlights those who are shaping the future of the real estate sector.

data.title: 74Software reports H1 2025 revenue of €344m, up 6.5% organically. Profit on operating activities spikes 585bps to 12.0% of revenue. ARR increases by 11.8% at Axway & 10.9% at SBS. Net profit for H1 2025 is €5.8m. Organizational strategies show firm positioning for long-term objectives.
data.description: 74Software reported a positive first half of 2025 with a revenue of €344.0 million, showing 6.2% total growth and 6.5% organic growth. Axway revenue was up 8.9% at €160.8 million, and SBS revenue increased by 5.0% to €184.2 million. Operating profit increased significantly, reaching €41.3 million, a 585bps rise to 12.0% of revenue. Axway's annual recurring revenue (ARR) grew by 11.8%, reaching €255.9 million, while SBS's ARR increased by 10.9% to €233.3 million. Product revenue increased to €280.0 million, with recurring revenue at €258.0 million. Services revenue was at €64.0 million. Net profit for the period was reported at €5.8 million, compared to a loss of €15.6 million in the previous year. This growth was fueled by strong performance across all product lines, including Managed File Transfer, B2B Integration, API Management for Axway, and Financing, Modular, Integrated, and Banking Components for SBS. The company's strategic repositioning has shown effectiveness, as evidenced by revenue predictability and resilience. The company has also managed to deleverage significantly, with net debt standing at €191.8 million. CEO Patrick Donovan stated that the company's strong start to the year reaffirmed its strategic direction and ability to execute plans, with a focus on long-term value creation.

data.title: UNI climbs to $10.33 (+2.92% in 24h) fueled by whale accumulation, breaking key $10 resistance. Bullish indicators suggest more gains ahead.
data.description: The cryptocurrency token UNI is currently trading at $10.33, marking a 2.92% increase in the last 24 hours following a rally primarily led by influential holders. This surge has propelled the token past the significant $10 resistance level. Technical analysis suggests a strong upward trend, indicating the possibility of additional price growth in the near future.

data.title: North Island Credit Union partners with Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater San Diego to provide backpacks & supplies for 100s of students.
data.description: In a collaborative effort for the fifth consecutive year, North Island Credit Union and the Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater San Diego have joined forces to provide support to local students. This initiative aims to alleviate the back-to-school stress for hundreds of Club kids in the San Diego area. The partnership, announced on July 24, 2025, underscores a shared commitment to community welfare and education. Through this program, a significant number of students will benefit from the resources and assistance offered by the two organizations.

data.title: ESOP Association pushing U.S. Senate to confirm Daniel Aronowitz as Assistant Secretary of Labor for EBSA.
data.description: A business group, The ESOP Association, is urging the U.S. Senate to quickly approve Daniel Aronowitz as the Assistant Secretary of Labor for the Employee Benefits Security Administration (EBSA).This position is crucial for overseeing various labor regulations and protections. The appointment of Aronowitz is seen as important for the smooth functioning of labor and benefits aspects. It is essential for ensuring the well-being of workers and upholding their rights. The confirmation process involves a thorough review of Aronowitz's qualifications and is vital for the effective administration of labor-related matters.

data.title: Community Heritage Financial, Inc. (OTC PK: CMHF) reported record net income of $2.5M for Q2 2025, up 31.1% from Q1 2025 and 71.2% from Q2 2024. Total assets reached $1.1 billion, up $4.2M since June 30, 2024, and $75.4M since December 31, 2024. Loan balance outstanding grew to $872.1M. Net interest income was $8.8M for Q2 2025. Noninterest income increased $311k in Q2 2025. Nonperforming assets totaled 0.13% of total assets as of June 30, 2025. Dividend of $0.08 per share declared on July 18, 2025.
data.description: Community Heritage Financial, Inc. reported record net income of $2.5 million in the second quarter of 2025, a 31.1% increase from the first quarter and a 71.2% increase from the same period in 2024. Net income for the six months ended June 30, 2025, totaled $4.4 million, a 52.8% increase from the same period in 2024. Balance sheet assets totaled $1.1 billion, with a $75.4 million increase since December 31, 2024. Loan balances stood at $872.1 million as of June 30, 2025. Net interest income was $8.8 million for the second quarter of 2025. Noninterest income increased in the second quarter driven by mortgage banking revenue and earnings on bank-owned life insurance. Asset quality remained strong with non-performing assets at 0.13% as of June 30, 2025. A dividend of $0.08 per share was declared in July 2025. Noninterest expense decreased in the second quarter compared to the first quarter. Net charge-offs to average total loans were zero percent in the quarter ended June 30, 2025. The total assets as of June 30, 2025, were $1.1 billion.
